texto: Here, Mortimer Wheeler thought, is power. And a reminder of our mortality.
explicacao: Citação de Robert M. Edsel sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Here, Mortimer Wheeler thought, is power. And a reminder of our mortality.” articula reflexao, mortality, mortimer em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Destiny is not one push, she thought as she waited to cross a quiet street on that cold Paris evening years later, but a thousand small moments that through insight and hard work you line up in the right direction, like the magnet does the metal shavings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert M. Edsel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Destiny is not one push, she thought as she waited to cross a quiet street on that cold Paris eve…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, destiny, thought pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e reflexao.

frase_original: Destiny is not one push, she thought as she waited to cross a quiet street on that cold Paris evening years later, but a thousand small moments that through insight and hard work you line up in the right direction, like the magnet does the metal shavings.

texto: The thought came back to him, as it often did: To save the culture of your allies is a small thing. To cherish the culture of your enemy, to risk your life and the life of other men to save it, to give it all back to them as soon as the battle was won … it was unheard of, but that was exactly what Walker Hancock and the other Monuments Men intended to do.
explicacao: Citação de Robert M.
